text:
Market today: Sentiment is shaky with "Korean crash" ripple effects spreading across Asia, signaling a tough week for retail. However, AI continues to be a key driver for tech, with new Chinese models and global partnership pitches.

Your holdings: SOXL (-44.02%) and SPAL (-41.51%) are deep in the red and highly leveraged; these need immediate attention. Your margin amplifies these drops. QQQ (-1.22%) and SMH (-10.6%) are also flagged for trimming.

Watch: Monitor broader market weakness from Asia and any follow-through on AI developments. Keep an eye on your leveraged positions for further decay.

Bottom line: Focus on aggressive risk management and protecting capital in this volatile environment.
plays: SOXL / SPAL: Given deep losses and leveraged decay, review your stops on SOXL and SPAL to prevent further erosion. Consider trimming if your risk tolerance is breached.
QQQ / SMH: Respect scanner's TRIM status; consider reducing exposure in QQQ if it approaches its 670 stop, and SMH near its 540 stop.
NVDA: For new tech exposure, NVDA shows a scanner BUY at 206.55-206.25 with a stop at 205.54, supported by positive AI news.
